title: Textile process operatives
description: Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, such as welding, weaving, pottery, and needlecraft. Apply plasterboard or other wallboard to ceilings or interior walls of buildings. Apply or mount acoustical tiles or blocks, strips, or sheets of shock-absorbing materials to ceilings and walls of buildings to reduce or reflect sound. Materials may be of decorative quality. Includes lathers who fasten wooden, metal, or rockboard lath to walls, ceilings, or partitions of buildings to provide support base for plaster, fireproofing, or acoustical material. Seal joints between plasterboard or other wallboard to prepare wall surface for painting or papering. (Based on 47 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Seal joints between plasterboard or other wallboard to prepare wall surfaces for painting or papering.
Fasten metal or rockboard lath to walls, ceilings, or partitions of buildings using nails, screws, staples, or wire ties.
Create decorative textures in finish coat using brushes, trowels, sand, pebbles, or stones.
Mix mortar and plaster to desired consistency or direct workers who perform mixing.
Apply coats of plaster or stucco to walls, ceilings, or partitions using trowels, brushes, or spray guns.
Nail channels or wood furring strips to surfaces to provide mounting for tiles.
Apply cement to backs of tiles and press tiles into place, aligning them with layout marks or joints.
Install metal lath where plaster applications will be exposed to weather or water, or for curved surfaces.
Install horizontal and vertical metal or wooden studs to frames so that wallboard can be attached.
Hang drywall panels on metal frameworks of walls and ceilings using lifts or hoists to adjust panel heights.
Clean and prepare surfaces for applications of plaster, cement, stucco, or similar materials.
Apply weatherproof, decorative coverings to exterior surfaces of buildings by troweling or spraying stucco.
Attach loads to rigging to provide support or prepare them for moving using hand and power tools.
Monitor machine operations and control panels to detect malfunctions such as clogged bushings and defective binder applicators.
Weigh ingredients, such as dye, to be mixed together for use in textile processing.
Observe bobbins as they are winding and cut threads to remove loaded bobbins, using knives.
Measure bobbins periodically and adjust tension if they are not of specified size.
Install, level, and align machine components to set up machinery for operation.
Tend spinning frames that draw out and twist roving or sliver into yarn, and monitor operations to detect defects.
Unwind lengths of yarn, thread, or twine from spools and wind onto bobbins.
Thread yarn, thread, or fabric through guides, needles, and rollers of machines for textile processes.
Adjust machine settings to produce products that meet specifications and to ensure equipment operates efficiently.
Start machines, monitor operations, and make adjustments as needed; inspect machinery to determine whether repairs are needed.
Stop machines when specified amounts of products have been produced and notify supervisors of equipment malfunctions.
Record production data, such as quantities and types of bobbins wound, and maintain logs of instrument readings.
Inspect products to verify that they meet specifications; remove defects in cloth by cutting and pulling out filling.
Monitor gauges and recording instruments to ensure that specified conditions are maintained.
Conduct minor repairs and maintain equipment using hand tools and lubricants.
Clean, oil, and lubricate machines and work areas, using air hoses, cleaning solutions, and hand tools.
Transport materials, supplies, and finished products between storage and work areas using carts or forklifts.
Load equipment receptacles or conveyors with material to be processed, by hand or using hoists.
Calculate amounts of materials to be loaded into furnaces, adjusting amounts as necessary for specific conditions.
Operate machines to process materials in compliance with applicable safety, energy, or environmental regulations.
Examine and measure completed materials or products to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring devices such as tape measures or gauges.
Monitor machine operations to detect malfunctions and report or resolve problems.
Record gauge readings, test results, and shift production in log books, including data such as meter readings and quantities produced.
Read work orders, specifications, or instructions to determine materials, procedures, and adjustments needed for machines.
Load and unload items from machines, conveyors, and conveyances, using hand tools and moving devices.
Examine products to verify conformance to quality standards and reject defective products that do not meet specifications.
Clean and maintain machinery, equipment, and work areas to ensure proper functioning and safe working conditions.
Measure dimensions of products, and monitor and record flow meter performance to ensure compliance with specifications.
Remove defects, such as chips or burrs, and perform minor repairs to machines when necessary.
Tend accessory equipment, such as pumps and conveyors, to move materials through production processes.
Weigh or measure materials or products to ensure conformance to specifications and prepare raw materials for processing.
Signal coworkers to direct them during the production process and record production operational data accordingly.
Stack or arrange tested products for further processing, shipping, or packaging.
Monitor production operations or equipment to ensure conformance to specifications, making necessary process or assembly adjustments.
Inspect articles for defects, and remove damaged or worn parts, using hand tools.
Repair and recondition leather products such as trunks, luggage, shoes, saddles, belts, purses, and baseball gloves.
Align and stitch or glue materials such as fabric, fleece, leather, or wood, to join parts.
Construct, decorate, or repair leather products according to specifications, using sewing machines and hand tools.
Dye, soak, polish, paint, stitch, or engrave leather or other materials to obtain desired effects or decorations.
Measure, examine, or test completed units to check for defects and ensure conformance to specifications, using precision instruments.
Operate equipment to verify operational efficiency and monitor machine operation.
Start machines and turn handwheels to engage feeding, cooling, and lubricating mechanisms, ensuring smooth operations.
Operate gas or electric kilns to fire pottery pieces and perform test-fires to determine specific colors and textures.
Raise and shape clay into wares on revolving wheels, using hands, fingers, and thumbs for shaping.
Prepare work for sale or exhibition, and maintain relationships with retail networks for facilitating sales.
Pack and ship pottery to stores or galleries for retail sale, ensuring safe delivery.
Inspect, weigh, and measure products to verify conformance to specifications, using instruments such as micrometers and rulers.
Select sizes and types of molds according to instructions or design and create glass objects using blowpipes and artisans' hand tools and equipment.
Patch broken edges or fractures using clay or plaster, and smooth surfaces of molds using scraping tools or sandpaper.
Verify dimensions of products using measuring instruments or inspect and measure finished workpieces to determine conformance to specifications.
Guide nozzles over stone, following stencil outlines, or chip along marks to create designs, and remove excess materials from mold mixtures.
Measure and cut products to specified dimensions using measuring and cutting instruments, and adjust properties of materials according to requirements.
Program computer numerical control machines and set up, operate, or tend presses and forging machines for various operations.
Inspect or measure machined parts to ensure conformance to product specifications and examine completed workpieces for defects.
Set up, operate, or tend machines to saw, cut, bend, or straighten metal or plastic materials, and load materials into machines for fabrication.
Turn controls to set cutting speeds, feed rates, or depths of cuts according to product specifications and observe machine operations for malfunctions.
Clean and lubricate machines, and maintain stocks of machine parts and machining tools.
Select and install cutting tools according to specifications, and replace worn tools or sharpen cutting tools as needed.
Clean work areas and maintain equipment storage to ensure that inventory is protected.
Monitor machine cycles and operations to detect jamming and ensure conformity to specifications.
Adjust and correct machine setups to reduce thicknesses, reshape products, and eliminate defects.
Operate shears, grinders, and rolling machines to modify steel and metal sections as per specifications.
Sort materials such as metals, glass, wood, paper, or plastics into appropriate containers for recycling.
Read work orders and blueprints to determine setup specifications and material needs.
Signal and assist other workers to position equipment, fill hoppers, and feed materials into machines.
Maintain machine coolant systems and check lubrication to ensure proper operation during machining.
Lift workpieces to machines manually or using hoists or cranes, ensuring safe handling practices.
Input dimensions into machine control panels and set machine feeds and speeds to ensure desired outcomes.
Disassemble and store parts from machinery as needed and perform minor repairs or preventative maintenance.
